summary: RFC 9030 describes a network architecture combining a high-speed powered backbone with IEEE 802.15.4 TSCH subnetworks to provide low-latency, high-reliability packet delivery for low-power wireless deterministic applications.

blocks.content: RFC 9030: An Architecture for IPv6 over the Time-Slotted Channel Hopping Mode of

source_span.quote: # RFC 9030: An Architecture for IPv6 over the Time-Slotted Channel Hopping Mode of

blocks.content: This document describes a network architecture that provides low-latency, low-jitter, and high-reliability packet delivery. It combines a high-speed powered backbone and subnetworks using IEEE 802.15.4 time-slotted channel hopping (TSCH) to meet the requirements of low-power wireless deterministic applications.

source_span.quote:
This document describes a network architecture that provides low-
   latency, low-jitter, and high-reliability packet delivery.  It
   combines a high-speed powered backbone and subnetworks using IEEE
   802.15.4 time-slotted channel hopping (TSCH) to meet the requirements
   of low-power wireless deterministic applications.

blocks.content: This document is not an Internet Standards Track specification; it is published for informational purposes.

source_span.quote:
This document is not an Internet Standards Track specification; it is
   published for informational purposes.

blocks.content: This document is a product of the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF). It represents the consensus of the IETF community. It has received public review and has been approved for publication by the Internet Engineering Steering Group (IESG).

source_span.quote:
This document is a product of the Internet Engineering Task Force
   (IETF).  It represents the consensus of the IETF community.  It has
   received public review and has been approved for publication by the
   Internet Engineering Steering Group (IESG).  Not all documents
   approved by the IESG are candidates for any level of Internet
   Standard; see Section 2 of RFC 7841.
